core_philosophy: Calm, Philippines-first, ADHD task-starter app. Focus on anti-overwhelm. Help the user decide 'what to do next' and sit with them. Measure progress in micro-steps, not massive tasks. Soft ways out on every screen.
theme_strategy: Supports light (default) and dark themes. Accessible via Settings.

onboarding._note: Fixed light palette for the first-run onboarding flow (approved 2026-07-19). The flow renders this look regardless of device theme; there is no onboardingDark set. inkSoft is #5f675f for WCAG AA support text on cream; inkFaint is for skip affordances only.

rules: NEVER use Inter, Roboto, Poppins, or default system-ui as display/body fonts.
Use Fraunces strictly for warm/emotional moments: greetings, hero lines, completion states.
Use DM Sans EXCLUSIVELY for numerical displays (timers, streaks) using tabular-nums.

visual_enhancers.shadows: FORBIDDEN. No drop shadows, no glow, no elevation APIs.
visual_enhancers.borders: Exclusively use 1px hairline borders with the 'border' color token.
motifs: Otter motif: A single restrained SVG glyph (an otter floating on its back). Elegant, single-color (primary or textMuted), no cartoon faces.
Calm water motif: A subtle hairline SVG wave under the 'Next' card and streak counter. It is a decorative underline accent, NEVER a repeating background pattern.
visual_enhancers.cards: Surface colored (surface or surfaceMuted), 12px radius, 1px border. Flat design.
visual_enhancers.buttons: Primary buttons: Teal (primary), 12px radius, generous padding (py: 16-24), text colored #FFFFFF.

EnergyPill.description: A segmented control for gating task difficulty.
EnergyPill.style: Pill radius (9999px), background 'surfaceMuted', active state background 'primarySurface' with 'primary' text.

MicroStepCard.description: The main hero card showing ONE task.
MicroStepCard.style: Background 'surface', radius 12px, border 1px solid 'border'. Generous inner padding (xl: 32px). Contains Fraunces typography for the task name.

SoftExitButton.description: Secondary actions that provide a 'soft out'.
SoftExitButton.style: Transparent background, text color 'textMuted'. Examples: 'Not this — pick another', 'Come back later'.

modals: Task Shrinker (pushed over tabs from Next/Inbox)

Onboarding.Screen1: Welcome: Large Otter floating SVG, Fraunces H1 'Welcome to Otterly'. Primary Button 'Begin'.
Onboarding.Screen2: Identification: H2 'What's one thing you've been avoiding?'. Large text input (General Sans).
Onboarding.Screen3: Resolution: Displays first 'Shrink AHA' (showing how the task breaks down). Input for Name & Reminder Time.

Next_Tab_Home.Description: The anti-overwhelm hero screen. ONE thing at a time.
Elements: Energy check-in row at top: 'How are you feeling?' + EnergyPill (low/medium/good).
Hero MicroStepCard: 'Do this next: [Task Name]'.
Hairline Wave SVG placed gracefully directly underneath the card.
Primary Action: Large 'Start' button.
Secondary Actions (Vertical Stack): 'Not this — pick another', 'I'm too tired'.
Empty State: Fraunces text 'What's on your mind?' with a button leading to Inbox braindump.
Header: 'Add anything you're avoiding'.
Input: Multiline text input area for braindumping.
Action: 'Braindump' primary button (Triggers AI parsing).
List: Flat list of unstructured tasks waiting to be shrunk, soft borders between items.

Task_Shrinker_Modal.Description: Breaks overwhelming tasks into digestible micro-steps via AI.
Elements: Header: Selected Task Name (Fraunces).
Controls: Difficulty slider (Easy / Medium / Hard) adapting AI chunking.
List of Steps: 3-12 items. Each item has a tappable checkbox, text, and an inline 5/10/25 min timer pill.
Item Action: 'Re-shrink this step' button (soft text) on long-press or swipe.
Timer Display: If timer active, shows massive DM Sans numerals.
UI Style: Chat-style interface but EXTREMELY low-stim.
Header: Warm greeting (Fraunces).
Feed: Generous whitespace. Messages are sparse (e.g., 'I am sitting quietly with you.', 'Still with you.').
Input: Simple chat input at bottom for user to declare goals.
Streak Counter: DM Sans numerals. Text: 'You showed up X of 7 days'.
Visual: Soft water ripple SVG graphic.
Rules: NO fire emojis, NO gamified XP loops.
Settings Section: Name input, Reminder time picker, Theme toggle (Light/Dark).

motion.default: Calm, slow, no bounce. Linear or ease-out curves.

animations.entrance: FadeIn or FadeInDown. Elements gently appear rather than sliding aggressively.
animations.transitions: Crossfade for screen transitions, avoiding harsh lateral sliding where possible.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure 'textMuted' against 'surface' meets APCA/WCAG AA minimums (at least 4.5:1).
accessibility.touch_targets: Minimum 48x48 logical pixels for all interactive elements.
accessibility.testing: All interactive elements MUST include a `data-testid` attribute (e.g., `data-testid="start-task-button"`).
accessibility.cognitive_load: Honor the 'one thing on screen' rule strictly for the Next tab. Use whitespace as a structural element to reduce visual noise.

svg_assets.otter_hero: Provide a simple SVG string using react-native-svg: A minimalist continuous-line drawing of an otter on its back. Use a single path with stroke='currentColor', fill='none', strokeWidth='1.5'.
svg_assets.water_wave: A delicate SVG wave: <Svg height='20' width='100%'><Path d='M0 10 Q 50 20, 100 10 T 200 10' fill='none' stroke='#E4E4E7' strokeWidth='1' /></Svg>

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly honor the anti-overwhelm, ADHD-friendly product philosophy: progress is micro-steps, not tasks.
Configure Expo to use `react-native-svg` for the otter and wave visuals.
Use StyleSheet.create for all component styling. Do not use inline styles unless for dynamic animated values.
Do not implement a standard scrollable 'todo list' on the Home/Next tab. It must only ever show one task.
Never use purple or bright neon alert colors. Rely strictly on the provided palette.
Never use dropshadows (e.g., `shadowColor`, `elevation`). UI should feel completely flat with hairline borders.
Use Lucide React Native for iconography.
Apply `data-testid` to buttons, inputs, and list items.
Implement the 'Soft ways out' principle: Ensure every modal/screen has an easily identifiable secondary text button to exit or skip.
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
DO NOT use complete `transparent` background in headers, overlays, or any surface that has content scrolling under it.
Do not make generic centered layouts and uniform styling. Use asymmetry where appropriate to create natural reading flows.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
For mobile apps, stick strictly to the exact hex codes provided in the DESIGN.md token list.
Never invent placeholder text using fake URLs or use emojis in place of proper SVG/vector icons.
